pi_items.source: Web search and public accessibility verification. BBC News Online is the official news website of the British Broadcasting Corporation, launched November 4, 1997. See: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BBC_News_Online

Web search and public accessibility verification. Reuters is the world's largest news organization with 2,500 journalists worldwide, owned by Thomson Reuters. See: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reuters

Web search and public accessibility verification. The Guardian is a major English-language newspaper published by Guardian News & Media, owned by the Scott Trust. See: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Guardian
Code analysis: Path is consistently referenced in task.json description, setup_task.sh (line 9), export_result.sh (line 64), and README.md (line 7). All references use identical path.

pi_items.metadata_value: Web Developer - cross-browser network performance testing and documentation
pi_items.verified_value: Web Developer - cross-browser network performance testing and documentation
pi_items.source: Task description states: 'You are a web developer hired to conduct a network performance audit'. README.md line 4 confirms context: 'Web developers regularly use browser DevTools to audit network performance across multiple sites.'

privileged_info_summary: The task uses three verified real-world news websites: BBC.com (British Broadcasting Corporation news portal, launched 1997), Reuters.com (global news agency with 2,500 journalists), and TheGuardian.com (major UK newspaper published by Guardian News & Media). The report must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/network_audit_report.txt. All three sites are legitimate, publicly accessible news portals. The task requires agents to use Microsoft Edge's Developer Tools to analyze network performance metrics (request counts and file sizes) for these live websites.
